input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

<current_datetime>2026-05-02T00:58:35.950Z</current_datetime>

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## AppThemeBinding in .NET MAUI

**XAML approach** — use `AppThemeBinding` to declare light/dark values inline:

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="15">

        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#E0E0E0}" />

        <Label Text="This page adapts to your system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#444444, Dark=#BBBBBB}" />

        <Button Text="Get Started"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

The framework reads the device's current theme and picks `Light` or `Dark` automatically. When the user toggles their system theme, bound values update in real time — no restart needed.

---

**C# alternative** — use `SetAppTheme` or `SetAppThemeColor` in code-behind:

```csharp
// For any BindableProperty:
myLabel.SetAppTheme(Label.TextColorProperty,
    Colors.Black,   // light
    Color.FromArgb("#E0E0E0")); // dark

// Shorthand for color properties:
myPage.SetAppThemeColor(ContentPage.BackgroundColorProperty,
    Colors.White,                // light
    Color.FromArgb("#1E1E1E"));  // dark

myButton.SetAppThemeColor(Button.BackgroundColorProperty,
    Color.FromArgb("#6200EE"),   // light
    Color.FromArgb("#BB86FC"));  // dark
```

---

**Key points:**

| | XAML | C# |
|---|---|---|
| Syntax | `{AppThemeBinding Light=…, Dark=…}` | `.SetAppTheme()` / `.SetAppThemeColor()` |
| Reacts live | ✅ | ✅ |
| Works on any `BindableProperty` | ✅ | ✅ |

- Both respond to `Application.Current.RequestedTheme` (`AppTheme.Light` or `AppTheme.Dark`).
- For larger apps, define theme colors in `ResourceDictionary` and reference them via `{StaticResource}` or `{DynamicResource}` instead of scattering hex codes.
- You can force a theme with `Application.Current.UserAppTheme = AppTheme.Dark;` to override the system setting.
data.reasoningText: The user is asking for a tutorial/explanation on how to use `AppThemeBinding` in .NET MAUI to support light and dark themes. This is a knowledge question — they want example code and explanation, not changes to an existing codebase. I'll provide a concise, practical answer.
